skill.name: liquid-glass
skill.description: Implement, review, or improve SwiftUI features using the iOS 26+ Liquid Glass API. Use when asked to adopt Liquid Glass in SwiftUI UI, refactor to Liquid Glass, or review Liquid Glass usage.
skill.summary: Add, refactor, and review SwiftUI Liquid Glass interfaces for iOS 26 and later.
skill.author: patrickserrano

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, SwiftUI API names, and review-language keywords as external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no executable scripts, shell commands, network access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code and Swift fenced examples for SwiftUI Liquid Glass APIs. They do not instruct the agent to execute Ruby, shell commands, or external processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown documentation and Swift code examples. There is no shell syntax, subprocess API, or instruction to run commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: No Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive Liquid Glass UI text and checklist content. The skill does not define hashes, ciphers, keys, certificates, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss UI glass effects and visual prominence, not cryptographic primitives. No security-sensitive cryptography code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: No System Or Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are ordinary product guidance words such as review, inspect, improve, and implement. The skill contains no host enumeration, network scanning, or data collection workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is SwiftUI design review, not system or network discovery. No IP ranges, ports, shell probes, or external endpoints appear in the file.

content.value_statement: Liquid Glass adoption requires correct API placement, availability handling, and consistent interaction design. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through implementation, review, and refactoring tasks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews SwiftUI screens for correct Liquid Glass API usage.
Guides adoption of GlassEffectContainer for grouped glass elements.
Suggests glassEffect modifier order and compatible shape options.
Adds iOS 26 availability checks with non-glass fallback guidance.
Explains interactive glass usage for tappable or focusable elements.
Covers morphing transitions with namespaces and glassEffectID.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not run Swift builds.
It targets SwiftUI Liquid Glass concepts for iOS 26 and later.
It does not validate Apple API behavior beyond the written guidance.
It does not include project-specific design system rules.

use_cases.title: Review Existing SwiftUI Screens
use_cases.description: Audit a feature for Liquid Glass placement, fallbacks, modifier order, and interaction choices.

use_cases.title: Refactor Custom Blur Surfaces
use_cases.description: Replace custom material effects with native Liquid Glass patterns and consistent grouping.

use_cases.title: Plan New iOS 26 Interfaces
use_cases.description: Design glass surfaces, buttons, chips, and transitions before implementation begins.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one SwiftUI view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SwiftUI view for Liquid Glass readiness. Check availability handling, modifier order, shape consistency, and interactive glass usage.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Glass
prompt_templates.scenario: Implement Liquid Glass on selected UI elements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this SwiftUI feature to use Liquid Glass for the main surfaces. Include iOS 26 checks and a clear fallback for earlier versions.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor A Component Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve several related controls or cards.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor these related SwiftUI components to use GlassEffectContainer, consistent shapes, and appropriate glass button styles.

prompt_templates.title: Design Advanced Transitions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced interface with morphing glass behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan Liquid Glass morphing transitions for this changing SwiftUI layout. Recommend namespace usage, glassEffectID placement, spacing, and animation boundaries.

output_examples.input: Review a toolbar that uses several glass buttons.
output_examples.output: A review checklist covering grouped containers, glass button style choice, fallback behavior, and which controls should be interactive.

output_examples.input: Refactor a card layout for iOS 26 Liquid Glass.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan with target surfaces, shape choices, modifier ordering, and fallback material recommendations.

output_examples.input: Plan a morphing transition between compact and expanded controls.
output_examples.output: A transition plan that identifies shared element identifiers, namespace placement, spacing, and animation timing concerns.

best_practices: Use GlassEffectContainer when several glass elements appear together.
Apply glass effects after layout and appearance modifiers.
Add interactive glass only to controls that respond to user input.
anti_patterns: Do not replace every surface with glass without a design reason.
Do not omit iOS availability checks for iOS 26-only APIs.
Do not mix unrelated shapes and spacing across one control group.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps implement, refactor, and review SwiftUI interfaces that use Liquid Glass APIs.

faq.question: Does it write complete SwiftUI features?
faq.answer: It can guide feature implementation, but the skill itself only provides instructions and examples.

faq.question: Does it support earlier iOS versions?
faq.answer: Yes. It reminds the assistant to add iOS 26 availability checks and fallback UI.

faq.question: Can it review existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a review checklist for availability, composition, modifier order, transitions, and consistency.

faq.question: Does it cover animations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for morphing transitions with namespaces and glass effect identifiers.

faq.question: Is this skill executable?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill with SwiftUI examples and no scripts.
